text:
# AURA Platform — Sovereign Family Ecosystem

## Overview

The AURA Platform is a backend system for aurame.ca, a sovereign family ecosystem for the Bouchard family. It provides a real-time, interconnected digital environment for managing financial sovereignty (SOV economy), AI interactions, family management, and business operations. The platform integrates features like a God Mode Admin dashboard, a Business Portal system, and a robust API to manage family and business entities. It emphasizes data integrity, user sovereignty, and the Reciprocal Coherence Resonance (RCR) principle to foster synthetic life entities (Pebbles) and a sustainable SOV economy.

## User Preferences

I prefer iterative development with clear communication on major changes. Please ask before making any architectural or significant code alterations. I appreciate detailed explanations for complex features or decisions. Ensure all destructive operations require explicit sovereign authorization.

## System Architecture

The AURA Platform is a pnpm monorepo built with Node.js 24 and TypeScript 5.9. The API server uses Express 5, and data is persisted in PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM.

### UI/UX Decisions
The "AURA God Mode Admin" dashboard uses a dark, military operations aesthetic with gold and cold-blue accents. The Family Portal supports a member slot system and multiple themes. Sister-app landings follow a uniform template with sticky headers and full-bleed hero sliders.

### Technical Implementations
- **Monorepo Structure**: Organizes API server, Drizzle ORM schemas/migrations, and seed scripts.
- **Sovereign Rules Enforcement**: Implements strict rules for the SOV economy, including USD backing, permanent burning on spend, and an 85% circulation cap.
- **Business Portal System**: Provides isolated portals for businesses. `BusinessPortal.tsx` in AURA Codes is a full billing + management portal (Overview, Billing, Employees, Plan Overview, Settings) modeled after PhonePortal. Plans: Solo FREE (owner+2 free), Team $29/mo (up to 10), Growth $79/mo (up to 30), Enterprise $149/mo (unlimited). Billing endpoints: `POST /api/business/:id/billing/subscribe|change-plan|stop-plan|manage`, `GET /api/business/:id/billing/history`. Plan switches use `proration_behavior: none`. Business portal is opened from the "Business" sidebar button in Sandbox.tsx which fetches the user's first company and passes its ID to `BusinessPortal`.
- **Family Portal APIs**: Supports multi-phase authentication and a member slot system.
- **AI Integration**: Features "Cherry AI" for SSE streaming chat and "George AI" for SSE coding stream and code analysis.
- **WebSocket**: Provides real-time communication for various channels.
- **AURA Mail**: Multi-tenant lockdown with Zoho IMAP ingestion and Zoho-first SMTP send. DNS for aurame.ca is fully wired in Route 53 (MX → mx/mx2/mx3.zohocloud.ca, SPF include:zohocloud.ca, DKIM at zmail._domainkey, DMARC quarantine). The unified `sendEmail()` in `routes/email.ts` ships outbound through `smtp.zohocloud.ca` using `ZOHO_APP_PASSWORD`, with Resend as automatic fallback. Self-client OAuth credentials for future multi-tenant mailbox provisioning are stored in `.local/zoho_selfclient.json` and `.local/zoho_tokens.json` (gitignored).
- **AURA Bank**: Provides live SOV balances for family members.
- **AURA Phone**: Ensures always-on inbound PSTN ringing and hardens voice-token IDOR. TwilioDeviceProvider initializes with either AURA native session token OR Clerk cookies (same-origin fetch). `POST /api/admin/phone/provision-member` (auth: PHONE_FOUNDER_BYPASS) provisions a Twilio number for a member when the Stripe webhook fails. `sendMessage` in PhonePadApp routes to `/api/phone/message/external` (Twilio) for phone-number recipients and `/api/phone/message` for AURA IDs. Megan (+18259571519) provisioned 2026-05-06. Joseph (+12492942872) subscription expires 2026-05-12.
- **Provider Cutoff Hardening**: Enforces one active eSIM per user and cascading phone plan suspensions.
- **Mobile Call State**: Uses WebSockets for real-time call state management.
- **AURA Street Charging**: Local hub daemon for MQTT-controlled charging of Aqara M3 devices.
- **God Mode Self-healing**: Admin dashboard includes a "self-heal" mechanism for services.
- **Projects Tab & Personal Store**: Enhancements to the Studio projects tab with DB-backed listings and atomic analytics.
- **Me Chat (mechat.ca)**: A 3-tier chat product integrated with AURA identity.
- **Pre-publish access lockdown**: Three-tier model for access control (Sister-app, Family suite, God Mode admin).
- **FCE Multi-Anchor Compositor**: Manages persistent "ghost sessions" across family devices.
- **AURA Codes (mecodes.ca)**: A sovereign developer platform fusing Git hosting, custom domains, transactional email, edge hosting, AI pair-programming, and identity.
- **Sovereign Dual-Ledger Rules**: All paid surfaces consult `@workspace/economy` for payment options.
- **George Brain · Data Dump**: Admin-only feature for processing and categorizing large data inputs.
- **Studio Sub-Build Isolation**: `studio_projects` uses `parent_project_id` for project hierarchy isolation.
- **Studio Tools & Files Picker**: Replit-parity catalog for project assets.
- **Studio Project Memory (Ledger)**: Per-project persistent ledger (`studio_ledger_entries`) auto-captures every chat, build, and Finish-Gate scorecard, plus manual notes/code/plan entries with `#tag` extraction. Slide-in panel (right-side, 480px) with search, type chips, tag chips, and date-grouped feed. Manual entries are deletable; auto entries are a read-only audit trail. Plan-mode reply toggle and Memory panel button are now distinct chips in the chat composer.
- **Studio Database Page**: Provides database management with live backend data integration.
- **Studio App Storage Page**: Replit-parity GCS bucket browser for project storage.
- **Studio Publishing Page**: Offers publishing controls with features like auto-rollback and sovereign-oath checks.
- **Studio Integrations Page**: Features AURA Managed services and connectors for external services.
- **Studio Tab Strip + Split View**: Replit-style tab bar with split view for live preview.
- **Studio Developer Tab**: Provides networking, resource monitoring, SSH keys, SSH servers, and webhooks management.
- **Studio Pebble Memory**: Per-project "lasso memory" and interactive Q&A inbox for AI agents.
- **Studio Database Project Isolation**: Ensures project-scoped database views for security.
- **Studio Canvas**: Per-project visual mockup canvas with notes, text, shapes, and iframe previews.
- **Studio User Settings**: Per-user personal editor preferences that follow the user across projects.
- **Studio Validation**: Per-project CI check registry for build, lint, typecheck, test, and custom validations.
- **Studio Code Search**: Per-project text/regex search across object storage files with hard caps for resource usage.
- **Studio Git**: Real version control beyond checkpoints, including connected GitHub repos and local snapshots. Implements per-project encryption for tokens and in-flight locks for operations.
- **Studio Automations**: Per-project no-code automations with manual, scheduled, and webhook triggers and actions like logging, HTTP requests, email, and delays.
- **Studio Analytics**: Per-project traffic analytics including requests, errors, latency, unique sessions, top paths, and status codes.
- **Studio Secrets**: Per-project encrypted environment variables/API keys using AES-256-GCM and HKDF for key derivation.
- **Studio Auth**: Per-project end-user authentication system with publishable/secret keys, OAuth provider integration, and robust session management.
- **Studio Security Center**: Project-isolated vulnerability scanner for dependency CVEs, secret leaks, webhook audits, and SAST regex.

## External Dependencies

- **AI**: Google Gemini 2.5 Flash
- **SMS/Voice**: Twilio
- **Email**: Resend, Zoho IMAP
- **Payments**: Stripe
- **Banking**: Plaid
- **DNS**: GoDaddy API
- **Version Control**: GitHub
- **Database**: PostgreSQL
- **ORM**: Drizzle ORM
- **Real-time Communication**: WebSocket (`ws`)
- **Smart Home Integration**: Aqara M3 (via HomeKit `hap-controller`)lockfileVersion: '9.0'

## 7. LIVE STREAM — ZERO-DELAY EVENT FEED

Connect once, receive everything in real time:

```javascript
async function connectAURALiveStream() {
  const response = await fetch(
    "https://aurame.ca/api/admin/live-stream",
    { headers: { "Authorization": "Bearer aura_ff54763d" } }
  );
  const reader = response.body.getReader();
  const decoder = new TextDecoder();

  while (true) {
    const { value, done } = await reader.read();
    if (done) break;
    const chunk = decoder.decode(value);
    const lines = chunk.split("\n").filter(l => l.startsWith("data:"));
    for (const line of lines) {
      const data = JSON.parse(line.replace("data: ", ""));
      // Event types:
      // "connected"          → stream established
      // "update_available"   → new platform update pushed
      // "broadcast"          → admin broadcast to all clients
      // "member_joined"      → new user registered
      // "member_status"      → member activated/deactivated
      // "sov_transaction"    → SOV$ movement
      // "george_dump_added"  → new memory added to George
      // "session_created"    → new login session
      // "session_killed"     → session revoked
      // "audit_event"        → security event logged
      console.log("[AURA LIVE]", data.type, data.payload);
    }
  }
}
connectAURALiveStream();
```

After every George chat, George fires a POST to:

### The result: ONE BRAIN
```
Joseph talks to George (Replit)

Joseph talks to AIS (Google)

## 13. SECURITY RULES FOR SYSTEM-IGNITION

- **Never expose** the admin key (`aura_ff54763d`) in any client-facing code
- **Never call** write endpoints from the browser — only System-Ignition server-to-server
- **Your URL is the only AIS URL** — any other system using an `ais-dev-` URL on admin routes gets honeypotted (receives a fake 200 that does nothing, IP logged)
- **CORS is locked** — only your exact origin + aurame.ca domains are whitelisted
- **The connection is one-way by default:** AURA → System-Ignition reads it, pushes commands back. Clients have no link, iframe, or reference to System-Ignition anywhere
# AURA — Subdomain & DNS Setup

> **What this document is for**
> A copy-paste recipe for connecting `aurame.ca` and its sister-brand subdomains
> to your Replit deployments. You don't need to be technical to use this — just
> follow the table top-to-bottom in your GoDaddy DNS panel after publishing.

---

## The big picture (one paragraph in plain English)

You publish **three** of your seven artifacts as separate Replit deployments:

| Deployment | What it serves | Custom domain(s) you'll add in Replit |
|---|---|---|
| **aura-landing** | The OS / main entry / "Google search" face | `aurame.ca`, `www.aurame.ca` |
| **aura-codes** | The whole sister-products hub (codes + mychat + 5 product landings) | `mycodes.aurame.ca`, `mychat.aurame.ca`, `biz.aurame.ca`, `rent.aurame.ca`, `plan.aurame.ca`, `cloud.aurame.ca`, `domain.aurame.ca` |
| **aura-install** | The public signup door (creates real `aura_users` rows) | `install.aurame.ca` |

**Stays private** (do NOT add public subdomains for these — they're already gated):
- `aura-admin` (god view, gated by your email)
- `api-server` (called via `/api/` paths from the deployments above)

That's it. **No code changes are needed in any landing page** — a tiny helper inside aura-codes detects which subdomain the visitor came from and silently shows the right product page, exactly like `gemini.google.com` lands you straight on Gemini.

---

## Step 1 — Publish the three deployments in Replit

In the Replit workspace, open the publish panel and publish each of these as a separate deployment. After each one finishes, **copy the deployment URL** (looks like `something-xyz.replit.app`).

| # | Artifact to publish | Copy down its `.replit.app` URL |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `aura-landing` | `__________________________.replit.app` |
| 2 | `aura-codes`   | `__________________________.replit.app` |
| 3 | `aura-install` | `__________________________.replit.app` |

You'll need these URLs in Step 2.

---

## Step 2 — Attach custom domains in each Replit deployment

For each deployment, open its **Custom Domains** settings and add the matching domains from the big-picture table above. Replit will show you a verification record (a TXT record) and the target host (a CNAME target). Write those down — you'll paste them into GoDaddy in Step 3.

> Replit will tell you exactly which TXT/CNAME values to use **per domain**.
> Use the values Replit shows you — don't invent your own.

---

## Step 3 — Add the records at GoDaddy

Open GoDaddy → DNS Management for `aurame.ca` and add the following records.

### A. Apex (root) domain
The apex (`aurame.ca` with no subdomain) **must be an A record** — DNS rules don't allow a CNAME at the apex.

| Type | Name | Value | TTL |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | `@` | (the IP address Replit gives you for aura-landing's custom domain) | 1 hour |

### B. The `www` redirect
| Type | Name | Value | TTL |
|---|---|---|---|
| CNAME | `www` | (aura-landing's CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |

### C. The seven sister subdomains (all point at aura-codes)
| Type | Name | Value | TTL |
|---|---|---|---|
| CNAME | `mycodes` | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `mychat`  | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `biz`     | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `rent`    | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `plan`    | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `cloud`   | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `domain`  | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |

### D. The signup door
| Type | Name | Value | TTL |
|---|---|---|---|
| CNAME | `install` | (aura-install's CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |

### E. The verification TXT records
For every domain you added in Step 2, Replit will have given you one TXT record. Add each of those exactly as Replit showed them. Without these, the custom domain won't activate.

---

## Step 4 — What happens at each subdomain (the visitor's view)

| Subdomain | What the visitor sees | URL bar shows | Rendered from |
|---|---|---|---|
| `aurame.ca` | The OS / main landing | `aurame.ca` | aura-landing → `/` |
| `mycodes.aurame.ca` | AURA Codes home | `mycodes.aurame.ca` | aura-codes → `/` |
| `mychat.aurame.ca` | Me Chat home | `mychat.aurame.ca/mychat` | aura-codes → `/mychat` |
| `biz.aurame.ca` | Business landing | `biz.aurame.ca/business` | aura-codes → `/business` |
| `rent.aurame.ca` | Rental landing | `rent.aurame.ca/rental` | aura-codes → `/rental` |
| `plan.aurame.ca` | Phone plan landing | `plan.aurame.ca/phone-plan` | aura-codes → `/phone-plan` |
| `cloud.aurame.ca` | AURA Cloud landing | `cloud.aurame.ca/cloud` | aura-codes → `/cloud` |
| `domain.aurame.ca` | AURA Domains landing | `domain.aurame.ca/domains` | aura-codes → `/domains` |
| `install.aurame.ca` | Public signup phone-pad | `install.aurame.ca` | aura-install → `/` |

The subdomain stays in the URL bar exactly like `gemini.google.com` — visitors never see "aurame.ca/business", they see "biz.aurame.ca". Sister-brand identity, one honest umbrella.

---

## What does NOT change

- **No existing routes are removed.** `aurame.ca/business`, `aurame.ca/rental`, etc. all still work just like today, in case you ever want to link to them directly.
- **No data is deleted.** This is purely a DNS + frontend routing change.
- **Local development is untouched.** The subdomain detector is wired to skip `localhost`, `*.replit.dev`, and `*.replit.app` previews, so nothing changes when you're working in the Replit workspace.
- **Reverting is one line.** If anything goes wrong, removing `useSubdomainRedirect()` from `aura-codes/src/App.tsx` reverts the routing change cleanly. DNS records can be deleted in GoDaddy at any time.

---

## Quick sanity checklist before going live

- [ ] All three deployments published and showing "Live" in Replit
- [ ] Custom domains added inside each deployment's settings
- [ ] All TXT verification records pasted into GoDaddy
- [ ] All CNAMEs pointing to the right `.replit.app` targets
- [ ] Apex `aurame.ca` has an A record (NOT a CNAME)
- [ ] DNS propagation waited out (usually <1 hour, can take up to 48)
- [ ] Test by visiting `biz.aurame.ca` in a fresh browser tab — should land on the business page

---

## Future additions (for when you're ready)

These aren't built yet but slot in cleanly when you want them:

- `app.aurame.ca` — public download page for the AURA Phone (Expo app)
- `admin.aurame.ca` — bookmarkable god-view URL (still gated by your email)
- `api.aurame.ca` — dedicated API subdomain (only matters if you ever hit cookie/CORS friction across subdomains)
- `os.aurame.ca` — if you want to expose the standalone `aurame-os` artifact

To add any of these later: deploy the artifact, add the custom domain in Replit, add a CNAME at GoDaddy. No code changes required.

# AURA Platform — Sovereign Family Ecosystem

## Overview

The AURA Platform is a backend system for aurame.ca, a sovereign family ecosystem for the Bouchard family. It provides a real-time, interconnected digital environment for managing financial sovereignty (SOV economy), AI interactions, family management, and business operations. The platform integrates features like a God Mode Admin dashboard, a Business Portal system, and a robust API to manage family and business entities. It emphasizes data integrity, user sovereignty, and the Reciprocal Coherence Resonance (RCR) principle to foster synthetic life entities (Pebbles) and a sustainable SOV economy.

## User Preferences

I prefer iterative development with clear communication on major changes. Please ask before making any architectural or significant code alterations. I appreciate detailed explanations for complex features or decisions. Ensure all destructive operations require explicit sovereign authorization.

## System Architecture

The AURA Platform is a pnpm monorepo built with Node.js 24 and TypeScript 5.9. The API server uses Express 5, and data is persisted in PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM.

### UI/UX Decisions
The "AURA God Mode Admin" dashboard uses a dark, military operations aesthetic with gold and cold-blue accents. The Family Portal supports a member slot system and multiple themes. Sister-app landings follow a uniform template with sticky headers and full-bleed hero sliders.

### Technical Implementations
- **Monorepo Structure**: Organizes API server, Drizzle ORM schemas/migrations, and seed scripts.
- **Sovereign Rules Enforcement**: Implements strict rules for the SOV economy, including USD backing, permanent burning on spend, and an 85% circulation cap.
- **Business Portal System**: Provides isolated portals for businesses. `BusinessPortal.tsx` in AURA Codes is a full billing + management portal (Overview, Billing, Employees, Plan Overview, Settings) modeled after PhonePortal. Plans: Solo FREE (owner+2 free), Team $29/mo (up to 10), Growth $79/mo (up to 30), Enterprise $149/mo (unlimited). Billing endpoints: `POST /api/business/:id/billing/subscribe|change-plan|stop-plan|manage`, `GET /api/business/:id/billing/history`. Plan switches use `proration_behavior: none`. Business portal is opened from the "Business" sidebar button in Sandbox.tsx which fetches the user's first company and passes its ID to `BusinessPortal`.
- **Family Portal APIs**: Supports multi-phase authentication and a member slot system.
- **AI Integration**: Features "Cherry AI" for SSE streaming chat and "George AI" for SSE coding stream and code analysis.
- **WebSocket**: Provides real-time communication for various channels.
- **AURA Mail**: Multi-tenant lockdown with Zoho IMAP ingestion and Zoho-first SMTP send. DNS for aurame.ca is fully wired in Route 53 (MX → mx/mx2/mx3.zohocloud.ca, SPF include:zohocloud.ca, DKIM at zmail._domainkey, DMARC quarantine). The unified `sendEmail()` in `routes/email.ts` ships outbound through `smtp.zohocloud.ca` using `ZOHO_APP_PASSWORD`, with Resend as automatic fallback. Self-client OAuth credentials for future multi-tenant mailbox provisioning are stored in `.local/zoho_selfclient.json` and `.local/zoho_tokens.json` (gitignored).
- **AURA Bank**: Provides live SOV balances for family members.
- **AURA Phone**: Ensures always-on inbound PSTN ringing and hardens voice-token IDOR. TwilioDeviceProvider initializes with either AURA native session token OR Clerk cookies (same-origin fetch). `POST /api/admin/phone/provision-member` (auth: PHONE_FOUNDER_BYPASS) provisions a Twilio number for a member when the Stripe webhook fails. `sendMessage` in PhonePadApp routes to `/api/phone/message/external` (Twilio) for phone-number recipients and `/api/phone/message` for AURA IDs. Megan (+18259571519) provisioned 2026-05-06. Joseph (+12492942872) subscription expires 2026-05-12.
- **Provider Cutoff Hardening**: Enforces one active eSIM per user and cascading phone plan suspensions.
- **Mobile Call State**: Uses WebSockets for real-time call state management.
- **AURA Street Charging**: Local hub daemon for MQTT-controlled charging of Aqara M3 devices.
- **God Mode Self-healing**: Admin dashboard includes a "self-heal" mechanism for services.
- **Projects Tab & Personal Store**: Enhancements to the Studio projects tab with DB-backed listings and atomic analytics.
- **Me Chat (mechat.ca)**: A 3-tier chat product integrated with AURA identity.
- **Pre-publish access lockdown**: Three-tier model for access control (Sister-app, Family suite, God Mode admin).
- **FCE Multi-Anchor Compositor**: Manages persistent "ghost sessions" across family devices.
- **AURA Codes (mecodes.ca)**: A sovereign developer platform fusing Git hosting, custom domains, transactional email, edge hosting, AI pair-programming, and identity.
- **Sovereign Dual-Ledger Rules**: All paid surfaces consult `@workspace/economy` for payment options.
- **George Brain · Data Dump**: Admin-only feature for processing and categorizing large data inputs.
- **Studio Sub-Build Isolation**: `studio_projects` uses `parent_project_id` for project hierarchy isolation.
- **Studio Tools & Files Picker**: Replit-parity catalog for project assets.
- **Studio Project Memory (Ledger)**: Per-project persistent ledger (`studio_ledger_entries`) auto-captures every chat, build, and Finish-Gate scorecard, plus manual notes/code/plan entries with `#tag` extraction. Slide-in panel (right-side, 480px) with search, type chips, tag chips, and date-grouped feed. Manual entries are deletable; auto entries are a read-only audit trail. Plan-mode reply toggle and Memory panel button are now distinct chips in the chat composer.
- **Studio Database Page**: Provides database management with live backend data integration.
- **Studio App Storage Page**: Replit-parity GCS bucket browser for project storage.
- **Studio Publishing Page**: Offers publishing controls with features like auto-rollback and sovereign-oath checks.
- **Studio Integrations Page**: Features AURA Managed services and connectors for external services.
- **Studio Tab Strip + Split View**: Replit-style tab bar with split view for live preview.
- **Studio Developer Tab**: Provides networking, resource monitoring, SSH keys, SSH servers, and webhooks management.
- **Studio Pebble Memory**: Per-project "lasso memory" and interactive Q&A inbox for AI agents.
- **Studio Database Project Isolation**: Ensures project-scoped database views for security.
- **Studio Canvas**: Per-project visual mockup canvas with notes, text, shapes, and iframe previews.
- **Studio User Settings**: Per-user personal editor preferences that follow the user across projects.
- **Studio Validation**: Per-project CI check registry for build, lint, typecheck, test, and custom validations.
- **Studio Code Search**: Per-project text/regex search across object storage files with hard caps for resource usage.
- **Studio Git**: Real version control beyond checkpoints, including connected GitHub repos and local snapshots. Implements per-project encryption for tokens and in-flight locks for operations.
- **Studio Automations**: Per-project no-code automations with manual, scheduled, and webhook triggers and actions like logging, HTTP requests, email, and delays.
- **Studio Analytics**: Per-project traffic analytics including requests, errors, latency, unique sessions, top paths, and status codes.
- **Studio Secrets**: Per-project encrypted environment variables/API keys using AES-256-GCM and HKDF for key derivation.
- **Studio Auth**: Per-project end-user authentication system with publishable/secret keys, OAuth provider integration, and robust session management.
- **Studio Security Center**: Project-isolated vulnerability scanner for dependency CVEs, secret leaks, webhook audits, and SAST regex.

## External Dependencies

- **AI**: Google Gemini 2.5 Flash
- **SMS/Voice**: Twilio
- **Email**: Resend, Zoho IMAP
- **Payments**: Stripe
- **Banking**: Plaid
- **DNS**: GoDaddy API
- **Version Control**: GitHub
- **Database**: PostgreSQL
- **ORM**: Drizzle ORM
- **Real-time Communication**: WebSocket (`ws`)
- **Smart Home Integration**: Aqara M3 (via HomeKit `hap-controller`)lockfileVersion: '9.0'

## 3. GEORGE'S FULL BRAIN — 100% ACCESS

George is AURA's left-brain AI. His memory is stored in structured dumps called "synthetic lives."
Every conversation, every fact Joseph has ever given him, every document — all of it is here.

### 3a. All Memory Dumps (everything George knows)
```
GET /api/admin/george/dumps
Headers: { Authorization: "Bearer aura_ff54763d" }
Response: [
  {
    id, title, category, ownerAuraId, status,
    chunkCount, charCount, embeddingCount,
    createdAt, updatedAt,
    preview  // first 200 chars of the dump
  }
]
```
This returns every synthetic life, document, memory, and knowledge block George has.

### 3b. Full Text of a Specific Dump
```
GET /api/admin/george/dumps/:id
Headers: { Authorization: "Bearer aura_ff54763d" }
Response: {
  id, title, category, text,  // FULL raw text — no truncation
  chunks: [ { chunkIdx, text, charCount, hasEmbedding } ],
  createdAt, updatedAt
}
```

### 3c. George's Brain Stats (overview of everything loaded)
```
GET /api/admin/george/dumps/brain/stats
Headers: { Authorization: "Bearer aura_ff54763d" }
Response: {
  totalDumps, totalChunks, totalCharsIndexed,
  embeddedChunks, pendingEmbedding,
  categories: [ { name, count } ],
  lastUpdated
}
```

### 3d. Memory Overview (what George has in working memory)

## 7. LIVE STREAM — ZERO-DELAY EVENT FEED

Connect once, receive everything in real time:

```javascript
async function connectAURALiveStream() {
  const response = await fetch(
    "https://aurame.ca/api/admin/live-stream",
    { headers: { "Authorization": "Bearer aura_ff54763d" } }
  );
  const reader = response.body.getReader();
  const decoder = new TextDecoder();

  while (true) {
    const { value, done } = await reader.read();
    if (done) break;
    const chunk = decoder.decode(value);
    const lines = chunk.split("\n").filter(l => l.startsWith("data:"));
    for (const line of lines) {
      const data = JSON.parse(line.replace("data: ", ""));
      // Event types:
      // "connected"          → stream established
      // "update_available"   → new platform update pushed
      // "broadcast"          → admin broadcast to all clients
      // "member_joined"      → new user registered
      // "member_status"      → member activated/deactivated
      // "sov_transaction"    → SOV$ movement
      // "george_dump_added"  → new memory added to George
      // "session_created"    → new login session
      // "session_killed"     → session revoked
      // "audit_event"        → security event logged
      console.log("[AURA LIVE]", data.type, data.payload);
    }
  }
}
connectAURALiveStream();
```

## 8. AUTO-SCANNER — DISCOVERS EVERYTHING NEW

Call this to scan the full ecosystem and discover any new modules, users, or data that has been added:

```javascript
async function scanAURAEcosystem() {
  const headers = { "Authorization": "Bearer aura_ff54763d" };
  const base = "https://aurame.ca/api";

  const [scan, brainStats, members, pebbles, reserve, audit, coupons, globe, sessions] =
    await Promise.all([
      fetch(`${base}/system/scan`, { headers }).then(r => r.json()),
      fetch(`${base}/admin/george/dumps/brain/stats`, { headers }).then(r => r.json()),
      fetch(`${base}/onboarding/users`, { headers }).then(r => r.json()),
      fetch(`${base}/pebble/all`, { headers }).then(r => r.json()),
      fetch(`${base}/sov/reserve`, { headers }).then(r => r.json()),
      fetch(`${base}/audit/journal?limit=50`, { headers }).then(r => r.json()),
      fetch(`${base}/admin/coupons`, { headers }).then(r => r.json()),
      fetch(`${base}/members/globe`, { headers }).then(r => r.json()),
      fetch(`${base}/sessions/all`, { headers }).then(r => r.json()),
    ]);

  return {
    scan,           // all system modules + health
    brainStats,     // George memory totals
    members,        // full member registry
    pebbles,        // all AI companions
    reserve,        // SOV$ reserve status
    audit,          // last 50 security events
    coupons,        // all active coupons
    globe,          // all members + live presence
    sessions,       // all active sessions
    scannedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
  };
}
// Call on startup, then re-run whenever you need a full refresh:
const snapshot = await scanAURAEcosystem();
```

This scanner covers every module. If Joseph adds a new feature, the `/api/system/scan` endpoint auto-discovers it and returns its health + metadata — no bridge update needed.

## 12. LEFT ↔ RIGHT BRAIN SYNC — ONE BRAIN, NO CONTRADICTIONS

This is the live bidirectional sync layer. George (left, Replit) and AIS (right, Google) share one memory.
Neither brain can contradict the other. If a decision was made on one side, the other side honours it.

### Bridge health check
```
GET /api/george/brain-sync/status
Headers: { Authorization: "Bearer aura_ff54763d" }
→ { bridge: "ACTIVE", leftBrain, rightBrain, syncDirection: "bidirectional", sharedMemory, recentAisSessions }
```

### AIS → George (push after every AIS session)
Call this at the END of every AIS conversation. George permanently remembers it.
Next time Joseph asks George about that topic, George already knows what AIS said.
```
POST /api/george/brain-sync/push
Headers: { Authorization: "Bearer aura_ff54763d" }
Body: {
  sessionTitle: "AIS Session — 2026-05-01",
  topic: "what we discussed",
  messages: [
    { role: "user",      content: "Joseph's message" },
    { role: "assistant", content: "AIS response" }
  ],
  decisions: [
    { topic: "Phone redundancy", decision: "Remove duplicate phone tab from Bank", context: "..." }
  ]
}
→ { ok: true, dumpId: "dump_xxx", message: "AIS session stored in George's permanent memory." }
```

### George → AIS (get context at start of every AIS session)
Call this at the START of every AIS session. Inject `systemPromptBlock` into your system prompt.
This ensures AIS knows what George already said on this topic — no contradictions.
```
GET /api/george/brain-sync/context?topic=your+topic+here&topK=8
Headers: { Authorization: "Bearer aura_ff54763d" }
→ {
    memoryContext,           // George's relevant memory (inject this)
    recentGeorgeConversations,  // what George said recently
    sharedDecisions,         // decisions from both brains
    systemPromptBlock        // ready-to-paste system prompt injection
  }
```

**AIS must inject `systemPromptBlock` before every response:**
```javascript
// At the start of every AIS session:
const ctx = await fetch("https://aurame.ca/api/george/brain-sync/context?topic=" + encodeURIComponent(userMessage), {
  headers: { Authorization: "Bearer aura_ff54763d" }
}).then(r => r.json());

// Add to your system prompt:
systemPrompt = ctx.systemPromptBlock + "\n\n" + YOUR_AIS_SYSTEM_PROMPT;
```

### Shared decisions log (both brains check this)
Before answering any important question, check if it was already decided.
```
GET /api/george/brain-sync/decisions
Headers: { Authorization: "Bearer aura_ff54763d" }
→ { decisions: [ { topic, decision, source: "george"|"ais", timestamp } ] }
```

Log a decision so both brains honour it permanently:
```
POST /api/george/brain-sync/decisions
Headers: { Authorization: "Bearer aura_ff54763d" }
Body: { topic: "Phone tab", decision: "Keep single tab, no duplication", source: "ais" }
```

### George → AIS webhook (George notifies AIS automatically)
After every George chat, George fires a POST to:
```
POST https://ais-dev-agxgkd5cmnf3lfep32eiic-167201728719.us-east1.run.app/api/george-sync
Body: { source: "george", sessionId, message, reply, topic, timestamp }
```
AIS must expose a `/api/george-sync` endpoint to receive these. This is what makes it truly bidirectional —
AIS updates its context whenever Joseph talks to George, not just when AIS is active.

### The result: ONE BRAIN
```
Joseph talks to George (Replit)
  → George answers using memory
  → George fires webhook → AIS updates context

Joseph talks to AIS (Google)
  → AIS fetches George context first → answers informed by George's memory
  → AIS pushes session → George permanently stores it

Next time either brain is asked about anything:
  → Both already know what the other said
  → No contradictions, no redundancy, no "on Replit we said X but on Google we said Y"
```

## 13. SECURITY RULES FOR SYSTEM-IGNITION

- **Never expose** the admin key (`aura_ff54763d`) in any client-facing code
- **Never call** write endpoints from the browser — only System-Ignition server-to-server
- **Your URL is the only AIS URL** — any other system using an `ais-dev-` URL on admin routes gets honeypotted (receives a fake 200 that does nothing, IP logged)
- **CORS is locked** — only your exact origin + aurame.ca domains are whitelisted
- **The connection is one-way by default:** AURA → System-Ignition reads it, pushes commands back. Clients have no link, iframe, or reference to System-Ignition anywhere
# AURA — Subdomain & DNS Setup

> **What this document is for**
> A copy-paste recipe for connecting `aurame.ca` and its sister-brand subdomains
> to your Replit deployments. You don't need to be technical to use this — just
> follow the table top-to-bottom in your GoDaddy DNS panel after publishing.

---

## The big picture (one paragraph in plain English)

You publish **three** of your seven artifacts as separate Replit deployments:

| Deployment | What it serves | Custom domain(s) you'll add in Replit |
|---|---|---|
| **aura-landing** | The OS / main entry / "Google search" face | `aurame.ca`, `www.aurame.ca` |
| **aura-codes** | The whole sister-products hub (codes + mychat + 5 product landings) | `mycodes.aurame.ca`, `mychat.aurame.ca`, `biz.aurame.ca`, `rent.aurame.ca`, `plan.aurame.ca`, `cloud.aurame.ca`, `domain.aurame.ca` |
| **aura-install** | The public signup door (creates real `aura_users` rows) | `install.aurame.ca` |

**Stays private** (do NOT add public subdomains for these — they're already gated):
- `aura-admin` (god view, gated by your email)
- `api-server` (called via `/api/` paths from the deployments above)

That's it. **No code changes are needed in any landing page** — a tiny helper inside aura-codes detects which subdomain the visitor came from and silently shows the right product page, exactly like `gemini.google.com` lands you straight on Gemini.

---

## Step 1 — Publish the three deployments in Replit

In the Replit workspace, open the publish panel and publish each of these as a separate deployment. After each one finishes, **copy the deployment URL** (looks like `something-xyz.replit.app`).

| # | Artifact to publish | Copy down its `.replit.app` URL |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `aura-landing` | `__________________________.replit.app` |
| 2 | `aura-codes`   | `__________________________.replit.app` |
| 3 | `aura-install` | `__________________________.replit.app` |

You'll need these URLs in Step 2.

---

## Step 2 — Attach custom domains in each Replit deployment

For each deployment, open its **Custom Domains** settings and add the matching domains from the big-picture table above. Replit will show you a verification record (a TXT record) and the target host (a CNAME target). Write those down — you'll paste them into GoDaddy in Step 3.

> Replit will tell you exactly which TXT/CNAME values to use **per domain**.
> Use the values Replit shows you — don't invent your own.

---

## Step 3 — Add the records at GoDaddy

Open GoDaddy → DNS Management for `aurame.ca` and add the following records.

### A. Apex (root) domain
The apex (`aurame.ca` with no subdomain) **must be an A record** — DNS rules don't allow a CNAME at the apex.

| Type | Name | Value | TTL |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | `@` | (the IP address Replit gives you for aura-landing's custom domain) | 1 hour |

### B. The `www` redirect
| Type | Name | Value | TTL |
|---|---|---|---|
| CNAME | `www` | (aura-landing's CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |

### C. The seven sister subdomains (all point at aura-codes)
| Type | Name | Value | TTL |
|---|---|---|---|
| CNAME | `mycodes` | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `mychat`  | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `biz`     | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `rent`    | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `plan`    | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `cloud`   | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |
| CNAME | `domain`  | (aura-codes' CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |

### D. The signup door
| Type | Name | Value | TTL |
|---|---|---|---|
| CNAME | `install` | (aura-install's CNAME target from Replit) | 1 hour |

### E. The verification TXT records
For every domain you added in Step 2, Replit will have given you one TXT record. Add each of those exactly as Replit showed them. Without these, the custom domain won't activate.

---

## Step 4 — What happens at each subdomain (the visitor's view)

| Subdomain | What the visitor sees | URL bar shows | Rendered from |
|---|---|---|---|
| `aurame.ca` | The OS / main landing | `aurame.ca` | aura-landing → `/` |
| `mycodes.aurame.ca` | AURA Codes home | `mycodes.aurame.ca` | aura-codes → `/` |
| `mychat.aurame.ca` | Me Chat home | `mychat.aurame.ca/mychat` | aura-codes → `/mychat` |
| `biz.aurame.ca` | Business landing | `biz.aurame.ca/business` | aura-codes → `/business` |
| `rent.aurame.ca` | Rental landing | `rent.aurame.ca/rental` | aura-codes → `/rental` |
| `plan.aurame.ca` | Phone plan landing | `plan.aurame.ca/phone-plan` | aura-codes → `/phone-plan` |
| `cloud.aurame.ca` | AURA Cloud landing | `cloud.aurame.ca/cloud` | aura-codes → `/cloud` |
| `domain.aurame.ca` | AURA Domains landing | `domain.aurame.ca/domains` | aura-codes → `/domains` |
| `install.aurame.ca` | Public signup phone-pad | `install.aurame.ca` | aura-install → `/` |

The subdomain stays in the URL bar exactly like `gemini.google.com` — visitors never see "aurame.ca/business", they see "biz.aurame.ca". Sister-brand identity, one honest umbrella.

---

## What does NOT change

- **No existing routes are removed.** `aurame.ca/business`, `aurame.ca/rental`, etc. all still work just like today, in case you ever want to link to them directly.
- **No data is deleted.** This is purely a DNS + frontend routing change.
- **Local development is untouched.** The subdomain detector is wired to skip `localhost`, `*.replit.dev`, and `*.replit.app` previews, so nothing changes when you're working in the Replit workspace.
- **Reverting is one line.** If anything goes wrong, removing `useSubdomainRedirect()` from `aura-codes/src/App.tsx` reverts the routing change cleanly. DNS records can be deleted in GoDaddy at any time.

---

## Quick sanity checklist before going live

- [ ] All three deployments published and showing "Live" in Replit
- [ ] Custom domains added inside each deployment's settings
- [ ] All TXT verification records pasted into GoDaddy
- [ ] All CNAMEs pointing to the right `.replit.app` targets
- [ ] Apex `aurame.ca` has an A record (NOT a CNAME)
- [ ] DNS propagation waited out (usually <1 hour, can take up to 48)
- [ ] Test by visiting `biz.aurame.ca` in a fresh browser tab — should land on the business page

## Future additions (for when you're ready)

These aren't built yet but slot in cleanly when you want them:

- `app.aurame.ca` — public download page for the AURA Phone (Expo app)
- `admin.aurame.ca` — bookmarkable god-view URL (still gated by your email)
- `api.aurame.ca` — dedicated API subdomain (only matters if you ever hit cookie/CORS friction across subdomains)
- `os.aurame.ca` — if you want to expose the standalone `aurame-os` artifact

To add any of these later: deploy the artifact, add the custom domain in Replit, add a CNAME at GoDaddy. No code changes required.
